methods.description: The Climate Leaf Analysis Multivariate Program (CLAMP) is a quantitative method used to reconstruct paleoclimate using woody dicotyledonous leaf architecture. It involves analyzing leaf characters such as margin, size, and shape, which correlate with climate, and using calibration datasets to reconstruct palaeoclimate.

steps.description: Five datasets are available for calibration, including Physg3arcAZ, Physg3brcAZ, PhysgAsia1, PhysgAsia2, and PhysgGlobal378.
steps.searchable_summary: access calibration datasets for CLAMP analysis

steps.description: PhysgAsia1 dataset was used because it is suitable for monsoon climate, encompassing 45 sites from China.
steps.searchable_summary: select PhysgAsia1 dataset for monsoon climate analysis

steps.description: Leaf characters were scored with the template sheets, followed by a table with percentage values of the leaf characters.
steps.searchable_summary: score leaf characters and calculate percentage values

steps.description: The analysis was conducted online with suitable calibration.
steps.searchable_summary: perform online analysis using CLAMP with selected calibration
